continuations.text: The Mona Lisa was painted by Leonardo da Vinci, a renowned Italian polymath of the Renaissance period. He began working on the painting in 1503 or 1504 and continued to refine it until his death in 1519. The Mona Lisa is one of the most famous and most mysterious paintings in the world, known for its enigmatic smile and the subject's elusive expression.

The Mona Lisa was painted by Leonardo da Vinci, a renowned Italian polymath of the Renaissance period. He completed the painting between 1503 and 1506. The full name of the painting is "La Joconde" in French. Leonardo da Vinci is celebrated for his skills in painting, sculpting, architecture, science, music, mathematics, engineering, literature, anatomy, geology, astronomy, botany, writing, history, and cartography.
